Klasse GameManager

java.lang.Object
boardLogic.GameManager
public class GameManager extends Object
Manages the game flow, including player turns, moves, and game state.

This class is a singleton and provides methods to start, run, and reset the game, as well as to handle player moves and game over conditions.

It also manages the game timer and provides functionality for saving and loading game states.

    Represents the various causes by which a player can lose the game.

  • Konstruktordetails

    • GameManager

      private GameManager()
      Private constructor to enforce singleton pattern. Initializes managers and sets up the game state.

  • Methodendetails

    • getInstance

      public static GameManager getInstance()
      Returns the singleton instance of GameManager.

      If the instance is not yet created, it initializes a new one.

      Gibt zurück:
      The singleton instance of GameManager.

    • initializeListeners

      public void initializeListeners(GUIBoard guiBoard)
      Initializes listeners for player moves and removals on the provided GUI board.

      This method sets up the move and remove listeners on the GUIBoard to capture player input.

      Parameter:
      guiBoard - The GUI board to which the listeners should be attached.

    • startGame

      public void startGame(String mode, DifficultyStrategy difficulty)
      Gets called when the startbutton gets pressed and initializes the game in a separate thread.

      Creates players according to chosen mode and sets the gameStatus to true, indicating the game is running.

      Adds an initial move to the history allowing to undo until the very start of the game.

      Parameter:
      mode - Can either be Singleplayer (Player and CPU) or Multiplayer (Two players).
      difficulty - The difficulty of the CPU player in Singleplayer mode.

    • runGame

      public void runGame()
      Controls the game loop, alternating between players and handling their moves.

      Waits for player input (or CPU moves) using getMoveInput(boolean) and processes the selected move. If a mill is formed, it waits for a stone to be removed via getRemoveInput(boolean).

      After each move (and removal, if applicable), it checks whether the game is over and saves the current state in the History.

      Important: This method may be run in a separate thread, which can be interrupted during waiting phases (e.g. when calling wait() for player input). In such cases, the input methods may return null. Callers must explicitly check for null values before accessing move arrays (e.g. move[0] or move[1]) to avoid NullPointerException.

      Siehe auch:
      • getMoveInput(boolean)
      • getRemoveInput(boolean)
      • saveMove()
      • resetGame()

    • getMoveInput

      private Node[] getMoveInput(boolean isPlayer)
      Helpermethod for runGame().

      Waits for a valid player input (via drag and drop) or performs the CPU move.

      Parameter:
      isPlayer - true if the current player is human, false if it's a CPUPlayer.
      Gibt zurück:
      A Node array [fromNode, toNode], or null if interrupted

    • getRemoveInput

      private Node getRemoveInput(boolean isPlayer)
      Helpermethod for runGame()

      Waits for the selection of an opponent's stone to remove, or lets the CPU automatically choose a stone to remove.

      Parameter:
      isPlayer - true if the current player is human
      Gibt zurück:
      The Node selected for removal, or null if interrupted

    • switchPlayer

      public void switchPlayer()
      Switches the current player to the opponent.

      Updates the timer for the new current player and resets the timer.

    • handleMove

      public void handleMove(PlayerEntity player, Node fromNode, Node toNode)
      Delegates the move done by a player to the current phase the player is in. Saves the last move and switches the player, if the move was successful.
      Parameter:
      player - The player making a move.
      fromNode - The origin node.
      toNode - The target node.

    • gameOver

      public void gameOver()
      Gets called when a loss condition gets fulfilled. Leads to the Game over screen with the winner as a parameter.

    • resetGame

      public void resetGame()
      Resets the game and prepares it to be started again.

      This method interrupts the current game thread to stop ongoing operations. As a result, methods waiting for player input in runGame() (such as getMoveInput or getRemoveInput) may return null.

      Callers of runGame() must therefore explicitly handle null values to avoid NullPointerException, especially when accessing move arrays (e.g. move[0]).

      Siehe auch:
      • runGame()

    • setWinner

      public void setWinner(PlayerEntity winner)
      Setter for the player that has won the game.
      Parameter:
      winner - The player that has won.

    • getCurrentPlayer

      public PlayerEntity getCurrentPlayer()
      Getter for the player that is currently able to perform a move.
      Gibt zurück:
      playerWhite or playerBlack

    • getOpponent

      public PlayerEntity getOpponent()
      Getter for the player that is not currently able to perform a move. Helper method for switching players.
      Gibt zurück:
      playerWhite if the current player is playerBlack and vice versa.

    • getNoMillTurnCounter

      public int getNoMillTurnCounter()
      Getter for the amount of turns that no mill was formed.
      Gibt zurück:
      The value of the noMillTurnCounter.

    • setTimePerTurn

      public void setTimePerTurn(int timePerTurn)
      Setter for the time a player has to perform a move before he loses the game.
      Parameter:
      timePerTurn - The new time a player shall have to perform a move.

    • getGameStatus

      public boolean getGameStatus()
      Getter for the current status of the game.
      Gibt zurück:
      true if the game is running, false otherwise.

    • getPlayerWhite

      public PlayerEntity getPlayerWhite()

    • getPlayerBlack

      public PlayerEntity getPlayerBlack()

    • startTurnTimer

      public void startTurnTimer()
      Starts a new timer with the set time per turn. Updates the timer label on every tick (time per tick set in the timer class). When the time runs out, the game is over and the current player loses.

    • stopTurnTimer

      public void stopTurnTimer()
      Stops the timer, if it is initialized correctly.

    • undoMove

      public void undoMove()
      Undoes a move, restoring the state before the last performed move, if there is a undo move.
      Siehe auch:
      • loadMove(Move)

    • redoMove

      public void redoMove()
      Redoes a move, restoring the state that was undone prior, if there is a move to redo.
      Siehe auch:
      • loadMove(Move)

    • saveMove

      public void saveMove()
      Adds the current state of the game to the History (Memento). This includes both players, the current state of the board, the number of moves no mill was formed and the time left for the move.

    • loadMove

      public void loadMove(Move move)
      Helper-Method for undoing or redoing a move. Resets all Parameters that got changed after performing a move.
      Parameter:
      move - The move that should be loaded. Can be an undo or redo move.
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the move is null.
      Siehe auch:
      • undoMove()
      • redoMove()

    • saveGame

      public void saveGame(File targetFile)
      Saves the current game state to a JSON file.

      This method creates a GameStateData object containing the current players, their phases, the board state, and other relevant information.

      After saving, it restarts the turn timer to ensure the game continues correctly.

      Parameter:
      targetFile - The target file to which the game state should be saved.

    • loadGame

      public void loadGame(File file)
      Loads a game state from a JSON file.

      This method reads the saved game state from the specified file and reconstructs the players, their phases, and the board state.

      After loading, it updates the GUI and starts the game thread to continue gameplay.

      Parameter:
      file - The JSON file containing the saved game state.
